free_text: Breakup of deuteron by impact of alpha particle and
deuteron
Institute for Chemical Research
- ANG1 is polar angle (lab.) between beam and proton
- MISC2 is azimuthal angle of proton
- Chemical-form of target is CD2.
- Physical-form of target is solid.
- Target-thickness is about 1 mg/cm2.
105 cm
Impulse approximation
Ichiko
- ANG1 is polar angle (lab.) between beam and proton
- ANG2 is polar angle (lab.) between beam and alpha
Beam intensity is 10 nA.
Detect proton and alpha in coincidence.
Solid angle: 1.3E-3 sr. for proton and 5.5E-4 sr. for
alpha
To detect the proton slit size 4 mm in dia.
with 1000-micron-thick
To detect the alpha slit size 2.1x3.2 mm in
dia. with 500-micron-thick
No information on source of uncertainties
